title: Discover the Unknown Biased Attribute of an Image Classifier
title_zh: 发现图像分类器的未知偏差属性
abstract: Recent works find that AI algorithms learn biases from data. Therefore, it is urgent and vital to identify biases in AI algorithms. However, the previous bias identification pipeline overly relies on human experts to conjecture potential biases (e.g., gender), which may neglect other underlying biases not realized by humans. To help human experts better find the AI algorithms' biases, we study a new problem in this work -- for a classifier that predicts a target attribute of the input image, discover its unknown biased attribute. To solve this challenging problem, we use a hyperplane in the generative model's latent space to represent an image attribute; thus, the original problem is transformed to optimizing the hyperplane's normal vector and offset. We propose a novel total-variation loss within this framework as the objective function and a new orthogonalization penalty as a constraint. The latter prevents trivial solutions in which the discovered biased attribute is identical with the target or one of the known-biased attributes. Extensive experiments on both disentanglement datasets and real-world datasets show that our method can discover biased attributes and achieve better disentanglement w.r.t. target attributes. Furthermore, the qualitative results show that our method can discover unnoticeable biased attributes for various object and scene classifiers, proving our method's generalizability for detecting biased attributes in diverse domains of images. The code is available at https://git.io/J3kMh.
summary: Recent works find that AI algorithms learn biases from data. Therefore, it is urgent and vital to identify biases in AI algorithms. However, the previous bias identification pipeline overly relies on human experts to conjecture potential biases (e.g., gender), which may neglect other underlying biases not realized by humans. To help human experts better find the AI algorithms' biases, we study a new problem in this work -- for a classifier that predicts a target attribute of the input image, discover its unknown biased attribute. To solve this challenging problem, we use a hyperplane in the generative model's latent space to represent an image attribute; thus, the original problem is transformed to optimizing the hyperplane's normal vector and offset. We propose a novel total-variation loss within this framework as the objective function and a new orthogonalization penalty as a constraint. The latter prevents trivial solutions in which the discovered biased attribute is identical with the target or one of the known-biased attributes. Extensive experiments on both disentanglement datasets and real-world datasets show that our method can discover biased attributes and achieve better disentanglement w.r.t. target attributes. Furthermore, the qualitative results show that our method can discover unnoticeable biased attributes for various object and scene classifiers, proving our method's generalizability for detecting biased attributes in diverse domains of images. The code is available at https://git.io/J3kMh.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究发现人工智能算法从数据中学习偏差。因此，识别人工智能算法中的偏差是紧迫而重要的。然而，以前的偏见识别流程过度依赖人类专家来推测潜在的偏见（例如性别），这可能会忽略人类未意识到的其他潜在偏见。为了帮助人类专家更好地发现人工智能算法的偏差，我们在这项工作中研究了一个新问题——对于预测输入图像的目标属性的分类器，发现其未知的偏差属性。为了解决这个具有挑战性的问题，我们在生成模型的潜在空间中使用超平面来表示图像属性；这样，原来的问题就转化为优化超平面的法向量和偏移量。我们在此框架内提出了一种新颖的总变异损失作为目标函数，并提出了一种新的正交化惩罚作为约束。后者可以防止发现的偏向属性与目标或已知偏向属性之一相同的简单解决方案。对解缠结数据集和现实世界数据集的大量实验表明，我们的方法可以发现有偏差的属性并实现更好的解缠结。目标属性。此外，定性结果表明，我们的方法可以发现各种对象和场景分类器的不明显的偏差属性，证明我们的方法在检测不同图像域中的偏差属性方面具有普适性。该代码可在 https://git.io/J3kMh 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自动发现图像分类器中未知偏见属性的方法，减少对人类专家的依赖。
AI.motivation: 现有偏见检测方法过度依赖人类专家预设潜在偏见（如性别），可能忽略未被察觉的底层偏见，需开发自动发现未知偏见的新方法。
AI.method: 利用生成模型潜在空间中的超平面表示图像属性，通过优化超平面法向量和偏置项，并引入总变分损失函数及正交化惩罚约束以避免平凡解。
AI.result: 在解耦数据集和真实数据集上验证了方法有效性，可发现多种物体和场景分类器中未被注意到的偏见属性，且具有跨领域泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能有效检测图像分类器中的未知偏见属性，为AI算法公平性分析提供新工具。

search_text: Discover the Unknown Biased Attribute of an Image Classifier 发现图像分类器的未知偏差属性 Recent works find that AI algorithms learn biases from data. Therefore, it is urgent and vital to identify biases in AI algorithms. However, the previous bias identification pipeline overly relies on human experts to conjecture potential biases (e.g., gender), which may neglect other underlying biases not realized by humans. To help human experts better find the AI algorithms' biases, we study a new problem in this work -- for a classifier that predicts a target attribute of the input image, discover its unknown biased attribute. To solve this challenging problem, we use a hyperplane in the generative model's latent space to represent an image attribute; thus, the original problem is transformed to optimizing the hyperplane's normal vector and offset. We propose a novel total-variation loss within this framework as the objective function and a new orthogonalization penalty as a constraint. The latter prevents trivial solutions in which the discovered biased attribute is identical with the target or one of the known-biased attributes. Extensive experiments on both disentanglement datasets and real-world datasets show that our method can discover biased attributes and achieve better disentanglement w.r.t. target attributes. Furthermore, the qualitative results show that our method can discover unnoticeable biased attributes for various object and scene classifiers, proving our method's generalizability for detecting biased attributes in diverse domains of images. The code is available at https://git.io/J3kMh. 最近的研究发现人工智能算法从数据中学习偏差。因此，识别人工智能算法中的偏差是紧迫而重要的。然而，以前的偏见识别流程过度依赖人类专家来推测潜在的偏见（例如性别），这可能会忽略人类未意识到的其他潜在偏见。为了帮助人类专家更好地发现人工智能算法的偏差，我们在这项工作中研究了一个新问题——对于预测输入图像的目标属性的分类器，发现其未知的偏差属性。为了解决这个具有挑战性的问题，我们在生成模型的潜在空间中使用超平面来表示图像属性；这样，原来的问题就转化为优化超平面的法向量和偏移量。我们在此框架内提出了一种新颖的总变异损失作为目标函数，并提出了一种新的正交化惩罚作为约束。后者可以防止发现的偏向属性与目标或已知偏向属性之一相同的简单解决方案。对解缠结数据集和现实世界数据集的大量实验表明，我们的方法可以发现有偏差的属性并实现更好的解缠结。目标属性。此外，定性结果表明，我们的方法可以发现各种对象和场景分类器的不明显的偏差属性，证明我们的方法在检测不同图像域中的偏差属性方面具有普适性。该代码可在 https://git.io/J3kMh 获取。 本文提出一种自动发现图像分类器中未知偏见属性的方法，减少对人类专家的依赖。 Zhiheng Li,Chenliang Xu cs.CV

authors_text: Léo Lebrat,Rodrigo Santa Cruz,Clinton Fookes,Olivier Salvado
title: MongeNet: Efficient Sampler for Geometric Deep Learning
title_zh: MongeNet：几何深度学习的高效采样器
abstract: Recent advances in geometric deep-learning introduce complex computational challenges for evaluating the distance between meshes. From a mesh model, point clouds are necessary along with a robust distance metric to assess surface quality or as part of the loss function for training models. Current methods often rely on a uniform random mesh discretization, which yields irregular sampling and noisy distance estimation. In this paper we introduce MongeNet, a fast and optimal transport based sampler that allows for an accurate discretization of a mesh with better approximation properties. We compare our method to the ubiquitous random uniform sampling and show that the approximation error is almost half with a very small computational overhead.
summary: Recent advances in geometric deep-learning introduce complex computational challenges for evaluating the distance between meshes. From a mesh model, point clouds are necessary along with a robust distance metric to assess surface quality or as part of the loss function for training models. Current methods often rely on a uniform random mesh discretization, which yields irregular sampling and noisy distance estimation. In this paper we introduce MongeNet, a fast and optimal transport based sampler that allows for an accurate discretization of a mesh with better approximation properties. We compare our method to the ubiquitous random uniform sampling and show that the approximation error is almost half with a very small computational overhead.
abstract_zh: 几何深度学习的最新进展为评估网格之间的距离带来了复杂的计算挑战。在网格模型中，点云和强大的距离度量是必要的，以评估表面质量或作为训练模型损失函数的一部分。当前的方法通常依赖于均匀随机网格离散化，这会产生不规则采样和噪声距离估计。在本文中，我们介绍了 MongeNet，这是一种基于快速且最佳传输的采样器，可以实现具有更好近似属性的网格的精确离散化。我们将我们的方法与普遍存在的随机均匀采样进行比较，结果表明，近似误差几乎是一半，计算开销非常小。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MongeNet的高效采样器，用于几何深度学习中的网格离散化。
AI.motivation: 现有的网格模型评估方法依赖于均匀随机采样，但这种方法会导致不规则采样和距离估计噪声较大，影响表面质量评估和模型训练。
AI.method: 本文引入了一种基于最优运输的快速采样方法MongeNet，能够实现更精确的网格离散化。
AI.result: 与传统的均匀随机采样方法相比，MongeNet在保持计算开销很小的情况下，将近似误差减少了近一半。
AI.conclusion: MongeNet是一种更高效、更准确的网格离散化方法，适用于几何深度学习中的表面质量评估和模型训练。

authors_text: Dmitriy Smirnov,Michael Gharbi,Matthew Fisher,Vitor Guizilini,Alexei A. Efros,Justin Solomon
title: MarioNette: Self-Supervised Sprite Learning
title_zh: MarioNette：自我监督的精灵学习
abstract: Artists and video game designers often construct 2D animations using libraries of sprites -- textured patches of objects and characters. We propose a deep learning approach that decomposes sprite-based video animations into a disentangled representation of recurring graphic elements in a self-supervised manner. By jointly learning a dictionary of possibly transparent patches and training a network that places them onto a canvas, we deconstruct sprite-based content into a sparse, consistent, and explicit representation that can be easily used in downstream tasks, like editing or analysis. Our framework offers a promising approach for discovering recurring visual patterns in image collections without supervision.
summary: Artists and video game designers often construct 2D animations using libraries of sprites -- textured patches of objects and characters. We propose a deep learning approach that decomposes sprite-based video animations into a disentangled representation of recurring graphic elements in a self-supervised manner. By jointly learning a dictionary of possibly transparent patches and training a network that places them onto a canvas, we deconstruct sprite-based content into a sparse, consistent, and explicit representation that can be easily used in downstream tasks, like editing or analysis. Our framework offers a promising approach for discovering recurring visual patterns in image collections without supervision.
abstract_zh: 艺术家和视频游戏设计师经常使用精灵库（对象和角色的纹理块）构建 2D 动画。我们提出了一种深度学习方法，以自我监督的方式将基于精灵的视频动画分解为重复出现的图形元素的解开表示。通过共同学习可能透明的补丁的字典并训练将它们放置到画布上的网络，我们将基于精灵的内容解构为稀疏、一致和显式的表示，可以轻松地在下游任务中使用，例如编辑或分析。我们的框架提供了一种有前途的方法，可以在没有监督的情况下发现图像集中重复出现的视觉模式。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自监督深度学习方法，将基于精灵的2D动画分解为解耦的视觉元素表示，用于后续编辑和分析任务。
AI.motivation: 传统精灵动画依赖人工标注和监督学习，缺乏自动发现重复视觉模式的能力，需开发无需人工干预的自监督方法。
AI.method: 联合学习透明贴图字典并训练网络将贴图放置到画布，生成稀疏且显式的视觉元素表示。
AI.result: 框架在无监督条件下有效分解 sprite 动画，实验验证了其在视觉模式发现及下游任务中的应用潜力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为图像集合中的无监督视觉模式发现提供了新思路，可应用于动画编辑和内容分析领域。

authors_text: Lucy Chai,Jun-Yan Zhu,Eli Shechtman,Phillip Isola,Richard Zhang
title: Ensembling with Deep Generative Views
title_zh: 与深度生成视图集成
comment: CVPR 2021 camera ready version; code available at https://github.com/chail/gan-ensembling
comments: CVPR 2021 camera ready version; code available at https://github.com/chail/gan-ensembling
abstract: Recent generative models can synthesize "views" of artificial images that mimic real-world variations, such as changes in color or pose, simply by learning from unlabeled image collections. Here, we investigate whether such views can be applied to real images to benefit downstream analysis tasks such as image classification. Using a pretrained generator, we first find the latent code corresponding to a given real input image. Applying perturbations to the code creates natural variations of the image, which can then be ensembled together at test-time. We use StyleGAN2 as the source of generative augmentations and investigate this setup on classification tasks involving facial attributes, cat faces, and cars. Critically, we find that several design decisions are required towards making this process work; the perturbation procedure, weighting between the augmentations and original image, and training the classifier on synthesized images can all impact the result. Currently, we find that while test-time ensembling with GAN-based augmentations can offer some small improvements, the remaining bottlenecks are the efficiency and accuracy of the GAN reconstructions, coupled with classifier sensitivities to artifacts in GAN-generated images.
summary: Recent generative models can synthesize "views" of artificial images that mimic real-world variations, such as changes in color or pose, simply by learning from unlabeled image collections. Here, we investigate whether such views can be applied to real images to benefit downstream analysis tasks such as image classification. Using a pretrained generator, we first find the latent code corresponding to a given real input image. Applying perturbations to the code creates natural variations of the image, which can then be ensembled together at test-time. We use StyleGAN2 as the source of generative augmentations and investigate this setup on classification tasks involving facial attributes, cat faces, and cars. Critically, we find that several design decisions are required towards making this process work; the perturbation procedure, weighting between the augmentations and original image, and training the classifier on synthesized images can all impact the result. Currently, we find that while test-time ensembling with GAN-based augmentations can offer some small improvements, the remaining bottlenecks are the efficiency and accuracy of the GAN reconstructions, coupled with classifier sensitivities to artifacts in GAN-generated images.
abstract_zh: 最近的生成模型可以简单地通过从未标记的图像集合中学习来合成模仿现实世界变化的人工图像的“视图”，例如颜色或姿势的变化。在这里，我们研究这些视图是否可以应用于真实图像，以有利于图像分类等下游分析任务。使用预训练的生成器，我们首先找到与给定的真实输入图像相对应的潜在代码。对代码应用扰动会创建图像的自然变化，然后可以在测试时将其集成在一起。我们使用 StyleGAN2 作为生成增强的来源，并研究了涉及面部属性、猫脸和汽车的分类任务的设置。至关重要的是，我们发现要使这个过程发挥作用需要几个设计决策；扰动过程、增强图像和原始图像之间的加权以及在合成图像上训练分类器都会影响结果。目前，我们发现，虽然基于 GAN 的增强的测试时集成可以提供一些小的改进，但剩下的瓶颈是 GAN 重建的效率和准确性，以及分类器对 GAN 生成图像中的伪影的敏感性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究利用生成模型生成图像变体并通过集成学习提升图像分类任务性能的方法。
AI.motivation: 生成模型能合成模拟真实变化的图像变体，但其在真实图像分类任务中的应用潜力尚未明确。
AI.method: 使用预训练的StyleGAN2生成器，将真实图像映射到潜在空间并施加扰动生成变体，通过测试时集成提升分类性能。
AI.result: 在人脸属性、猫脸和汽车分类任务中获得小幅性能提升，但生成图像的重建效率和分类器对生成伪影的敏感性仍是瓶颈。
AI.conclusion: 生成变体集成方法具有潜力，但需改进生成模型重建质量与分类器鲁棒性以实现更大收益。

search_text: Ensembling with Deep Generative Views 与深度生成视图集成 Recent generative models can synthesize "views" of artificial images that mimic real-world variations, such as changes in color or pose, simply by learning from unlabeled image collections. Here, we investigate whether such views can be applied to real images to benefit downstream analysis tasks such as image classification. Using a pretrained generator, we first find the latent code corresponding to a given real input image. Applying perturbations to the code creates natural variations of the image, which can then be ensembled together at test-time. We use StyleGAN2 as the source of generative augmentations and investigate this setup on classification tasks involving facial attributes, cat faces, and cars. Critically, we find that several design decisions are required towards making this process work; the perturbation procedure, weighting between the augmentations and original image, and training the classifier on synthesized images can all impact the result. Currently, we find that while test-time ensembling with GAN-based augmentations can offer some small improvements, the remaining bottlenecks are the efficiency and accuracy of the GAN reconstructions, coupled with classifier sensitivities to artifacts in GAN-generated images. 最近的生成模型可以简单地通过从未标记的图像集合中学习来合成模仿现实世界变化的人工图像的“视图”，例如颜色或姿势的变化。在这里，我们研究这些视图是否可以应用于真实图像，以有利于图像分类等下游分析任务。使用预训练的生成器，我们首先找到与给定的真实输入图像相对应的潜在代码。对代码应用扰动会创建图像的自然变化，然后可以在测试时将其集成在一起。我们使用 StyleGAN2 作为生成增强的来源，并研究了涉及面部属性、猫脸和汽车的分类任务的设置。至关重要的是，我们发现要使这个过程发挥作用需要几个设计决策；扰动过程、增强图像和原始图像之间的加权以及在合成图像上训练分类器都会影响结果。目前，我们发现，虽然基于 GAN 的增强的测试时集成可以提供一些小的改进，但剩下的瓶颈是 GAN 重建的效率和准确性，以及分类器对 GAN 生成图像中的伪影的敏感性。 本文研究利用生成模型生成图像变体并通过集成学习提升图像分类任务性能的方法。 Lucy Chai,Jun-Yan Zhu,Eli Shechtman,Phillip Isola,Richard Zhang cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Debidatta Dwibedi,Yusuf Aytar,Jonathan Tompson,Pierre Sermanet,Andrew Zisserman
title: With a Little Help from My Friends: Nearest-Neighbor Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations
title_zh: 在我朋友的帮助下：视觉表示的最近邻对比学习
abstract: Self-supervised learning algorithms based on instance discrimination train encoders to be invariant to pre-defined transformations of the same instance. While most methods treat different views of the same image as positives for a contrastive loss, we are interested in using positives from other instances in the dataset. Our method, Nearest-Neighbor Contrastive Learning of visual Representations (NNCLR), samples the nearest neighbors from the dataset in the latent space, and treats them as positives. This provides more semantic variations than pre-defined transformations. We find that using the nearest-neighbor as positive in contrastive losses improves performance significantly on ImageNet classification, from 71.7% to 75.6%, outperforming previous state-of-the-art methods. On semi-supervised learning benchmarks we improve performance significantly when only 1% ImageNet labels are available, from 53.8% to 56.5%. On transfer learning benchmarks our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods (including supervised learning with ImageNet) on 8 out of 12 downstream datasets. Furthermore, we demonstrate empirically that our method is less reliant on complex data augmentations. We see a relative reduction of only 2.1% ImageNet Top-1 accuracy when we train using only random crops.
summary: Self-supervised learning algorithms based on instance discrimination train encoders to be invariant to pre-defined transformations of the same instance. While most methods treat different views of the same image as positives for a contrastive loss, we are interested in using positives from other instances in the dataset. Our method, Nearest-Neighbor Contrastive Learning of visual Representations (NNCLR), samples the nearest neighbors from the dataset in the latent space, and treats them as positives. This provides more semantic variations than pre-defined transformations. We find that using the nearest-neighbor as positive in contrastive losses improves performance significantly on ImageNet classification, from 71.7% to 75.6%, outperforming previous state-of-the-art methods. On semi-supervised learning benchmarks we improve performance significantly when only 1% ImageNet labels are available, from 53.8% to 56.5%. On transfer learning benchmarks our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods (including supervised learning with ImageNet) on 8 out of 12 downstream datasets. Furthermore, we demonstrate empirically that our method is less reliant on complex data augmentations. We see a relative reduction of only 2.1% ImageNet Top-1 accuracy when we train using only random crops.
abstract_zh: 基于实例区分的自监督学习算法训练编码器对于同一实例的预定义变换保持不变。虽然大多数方法将同一图像的不同视图视为对比损失的正值，但我们有兴趣使用数据集中其他实例的正值。我们的方法，视觉表示的最近邻对比学习（NNCLR），从潜在空间中的数据集中采样最近邻，并将它们视为正例。这提供了比预定义转换更多的语义变化。我们发现，在对比损失中使用最近邻作为正值可以显着提高 ImageNet 分类的性能，从 71.7% 提高到 75.6%，优于以前最先进的方法。在半监督学习基准上，当只有 1% 的 ImageNet 标签可用时，我们的性能显着提高，从 53.8% 提高到 56.5%。在迁移学习基准测试中，我们的方法在 12 个下游数据集中的 8 个上优于最先进的方法（包括 ImageNet 的监督学习）。此外，我们凭经验证明我们的方法较少依赖于复杂的数据增强。当我们仅使用随机裁剪进行训练时，我们发现 ImageNet Top-1 准确率相对降低了 2.1%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于最近邻对比学习的视觉表示方法（NNCLR），通过在潜在空间中采样最近邻作为正样本，提升自监督学习的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统对比学习依赖预定义的数据增强作为正样本，可能缺乏语义多样性。本文旨在通过引入其他实例的最近邻作为正样本，增加语义变化以提升模型表现。
AI.method: NNCLR在潜在空间中找到每个样本的最近邻，并将其作为正样本用于对比损失计算，替代传统预定义的数据增强方式。
AI.result: 在ImageNet分类任务中准确率从71.7%提升至75.6%；半监督学习（1%标签）从53.8%提升至56.5%；迁移学习在8/12数据集上优于现有方法，且对数据增强的依赖降低2.1%。
AI.conclusion: NNCLR通过引入最近邻正样本，有效提升了视觉表示学习的性能，减少了对复杂数据增强的依赖，并在多个基准任务中取得优异结果。

authors_text: Anjana Deva Prasad,Aditya Balu,Harshil Shah,Soumik Sarkar,Chinmay Hegde,Adarsh Krishnamurthy
title: NURBS-Diff: A Differentiable Programming Module for NURBS
title_zh: NURBS-Diff：NURBS 的可微分编程模块
abstract: Boundary representations (B-reps) using Non-Uniform Rational B-splines (NURBS) are the de facto standard used in CAD, but their utility in deep learning-based approaches is not well researched. We propose a differentiable NURBS module to integrate NURBS representations of CAD models with deep learning methods. We mathematically define the derivatives of the NURBS curves or surfaces with respect to the input parameters (control points, weights, and the knot vector). These derivatives are used to define an approximate Jacobian used for performing the "backward" evaluation to train the deep learning models. We have implemented our NURBS module using GPU-accelerated algorithms and integrated it with PyTorch, a popular deep learning framework. We demonstrate the efficacy of our NURBS module in performing CAD operations such as curve or surface fitting and surface offsetting. Further, we show its utility in deep learning for unsupervised point cloud reconstruction and enforce analysis constraints. These examples show that our module performs better for certain deep learning frameworks and can be directly integrated with any deep-learning framework requiring NURBS.
summary: Boundary representations (B-reps) using Non-Uniform Rational B-splines (NURBS) are the de facto standard used in CAD, but their utility in deep learning-based approaches is not well researched. We propose a differentiable NURBS module to integrate NURBS representations of CAD models with deep learning methods. We mathematically define the derivatives of the NURBS curves or surfaces with respect to the input parameters (control points, weights, and the knot vector). These derivatives are used to define an approximate Jacobian used for performing the "backward" evaluation to train the deep learning models. We have implemented our NURBS module using GPU-accelerated algorithms and integrated it with PyTorch, a popular deep learning framework. We demonstrate the efficacy of our NURBS module in performing CAD operations such as curve or surface fitting and surface offsetting. Further, we show its utility in deep learning for unsupervised point cloud reconstruction and enforce analysis constraints. These examples show that our module performs better for certain deep learning frameworks and can be directly integrated with any deep-learning framework requiring NURBS.
abstract_zh: 使用非均匀有理 B 样条 (NURBS) 的边界表示 (B-reps) 是 CAD 中使用的事实上的标准，但它们在基于深度学习的方法中的实用性尚未得到充分研究。我们提出了一个可微分的 NURBS 模块，将 CAD 模型的 NURBS 表示与深度学习方法集成。我们以数学方式定义 NURBS 曲线或曲面相对于输入参数（控制点、权重和结向量）的导数。这些导数用于定义近似雅可比行列式，用于执行“向后”评估以训练深度学习模型。我们使用 GPU 加速算法实现了 NURBS 模块，并将其与流行的深度学习框架 PyTorch 集成。我们展示了 NURBS 模块在执行 CAD 操作（例如曲线或曲面拟合以及曲面偏移）方面的功效。此外，我们展示了它在深度学习中用于无监督点云重建和强制分析约束的实用性。这些示例表明，我们的模块对于某些深度学习框架表现更好，并且可以直接与任何需要 NURBS 的深度学习框架集成。

AI.tldr: 开发一个可微分的NURBS模块，将CAD模型的边界表示与深度学习方法结合。
AI.motivation: 传统NURBS在深度学习中应用不足，需通过可微模块实现CAD与深度学习的整合以提升性能。
AI.method: 数学定义NURBS参数导数，构建近似雅可比矩阵用于反向传播，并基于GPU加速实现PyTorch集成。
AI.result: 模块在CAD操作（拟合/偏移）及深度学习任务（点云重建/约束分析）中表现优异，可直接集成至主流框架。
AI.conclusion: 提出的NURBS-Diff模块有效桥接CAD与深度学习，为复杂几何建模与AI应用提供新工具。

ai_content.tldr: 开发一个可微分的NURBS模块，将CAD模型的边界表示与深度学习方法结合。
ai_content.motivation: 传统NURBS在深度学习中应用不足，需通过可微模块实现CAD与深度学习的整合以提升性能。
ai_content.method: 数学定义NURBS参数导数，构建近似雅可比矩阵用于反向传播，并基于GPU加速实现PyTorch集成。
ai_content.result: 模块在CAD操作（拟合/偏移）及深度学习任务（点云重建/约束分析）中表现优异，可直接集成至主流框架。
ai_content.conclusion: 提出的NURBS-Diff模块有效桥接CAD与深度学习，为复杂几何建模与AI应用提供新工具。

search_text: NURBS-Diff: A Differentiable Programming Module for NURBS NURBS-Diff：NURBS 的可微分编程模块 Boundary representations (B-reps) using Non-Uniform Rational B-splines (NURBS) are the de facto standard used in CAD, but their utility in deep learning-based approaches is not well researched. We propose a differentiable NURBS module to integrate NURBS representations of CAD models with deep learning methods. We mathematically define the derivatives of the NURBS curves or surfaces with respect to the input parameters (control points, weights, and the knot vector). These derivatives are used to define an approximate Jacobian used for performing the "backward" evaluation to train the deep learning models. We have implemented our NURBS module using GPU-accelerated algorithms and integrated it with PyTorch, a popular deep learning framework. We demonstrate the efficacy of our NURBS module in performing CAD operations such as curve or surface fitting and surface offsetting. Further, we show its utility in deep learning for unsupervised point cloud reconstruction and enforce analysis constraints. These examples show that our module performs better for certain deep learning frameworks and can be directly integrated with any deep-learning framework requiring NURBS. 使用非均匀有理 B 样条 (NURBS) 的边界表示 (B-reps) 是 CAD 中使用的事实上的标准，但它们在基于深度学习的方法中的实用性尚未得到充分研究。我们提出了一个可微分的 NURBS 模块，将 CAD 模型的 NURBS 表示与深度学习方法集成。我们以数学方式定义 NURBS 曲线或曲面相对于输入参数（控制点、权重和结向量）的导数。这些导数用于定义近似雅可比行列式，用于执行“向后”评估以训练深度学习模型。我们使用 GPU 加速算法实现了 NURBS 模块，并将其与流行的深度学习框架 PyTorch 集成。我们展示了 NURBS 模块在执行 CAD 操作（例如曲线或曲面拟合以及曲面偏移）方面的功效。此外，我们展示了它在深度学习中用于无监督点云重建和强制分析约束的实用性。这些示例表明，我们的模块对于某些深度学习框架表现更好，并且可以直接与任何需要 NURBS 的深度学习框架集成。 开发一个可微分的NURBS模块，将CAD模型的边界表示与深度学习方法结合。 Anjana Deva Prasad,Aditya Balu,Harshil Shah,Soumik Sarkar,Chinmay Hegde,Adarsh Krishnamurthy cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Bin Yan,Houwen Peng,Kan Wu,Dong Wang,Jianlong Fu,Huchuan Lu
title: LightTrack: Finding Lightweight Neural Networks for Object Tracking via One-Shot Architecture Search
title_zh: LightTrack：通过一次性架构搜索寻找用于对象跟踪的轻量级神经网络
abstract: Object tracking has achieved significant progress over the past few years. However, state-of-the-art trackers become increasingly heavy and expensive, which limits their deployments in resource-constrained applications. In this work, we present LightTrack, which uses neural architecture search (NAS) to design more lightweight and efficient object trackers. Comprehensive experiments show that our LightTrack is effective. It can find trackers that achieve superior performance compared to handcrafted SOTA trackers, such as SiamRPN++ and Ocean, while using much fewer model Flops and parameters. Moreover, when deployed on resource-constrained mobile chipsets, the discovered trackers run much faster. For example, on Snapdragon 845 Adreno GPU, LightTrack runs $12\times$ faster than Ocean, while using $13\times$ fewer parameters and $38\times$ fewer Flops. Such improvements might narrow the gap between academic models and industrial deployments in object tracking task. LightTrack is released at https://github.com/researchmm/LightTrack.
summary: Object tracking has achieved significant progress over the past few years. However, state-of-the-art trackers become increasingly heavy and expensive, which limits their deployments in resource-constrained applications. In this work, we present LightTrack, which uses neural architecture search (NAS) to design more lightweight and efficient object trackers. Comprehensive experiments show that our LightTrack is effective. It can find trackers that achieve superior performance compared to handcrafted SOTA trackers, such as SiamRPN++ and Ocean, while using much fewer model Flops and parameters. Moreover, when deployed on resource-constrained mobile chipsets, the discovered trackers run much faster. For example, on Snapdragon 845 Adreno GPU, LightTrack runs $12\times$ faster than Ocean, while using $13\times$ fewer parameters and $38\times$ fewer Flops. Such improvements might narrow the gap between academic models and industrial deployments in object tracking task. LightTrack is released at https://github.com/researchmm/LightTrack.
abstract_zh: 对象跟踪在过去几年中取得了重大进展。然而，最先进的跟踪器变得越来越重和昂贵，这限制了它们在资源受限的应用程序中的部署。在这项工作中，我们提出了 LightTrack，它使用神经架构搜索（NAS）来设计更轻量级和高效的对象跟踪器。综合实验表明我们的LightTrack是有效的。与手工制作的 SOTA 跟踪器（例如 SiamRPN++ 和 Ocean）相比，它可以找到性能优越的跟踪器，同时使用更少的模型 Flops 和参数。此外，当部署在资源有限的移动芯片组上时，所发现的跟踪器运行速度要快得多。例如，在 Snapdragon 845 Adreno GPU 上，LightTrack 的运行速度比 Ocean 快 12 倍，同时使用的参数少 13 倍，Flops 少 38 倍。这些改进可能会缩小目标跟踪任务中学术模型与工业部署之间的差距。 LightTrack 发布于 https://github.com/researchmm/LightTrack。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LightTrack的方法，通过一次性的神经网络架构搜索（NAS）来设计更轻量高效的物体跟踪器。
AI.motivation: 当前最先进的物体跟踪器变得越来越重且成本高，限制了它们在资源受限的应用中的部署。因此，需要一种方法来设计更轻量且高效的跟踪器。
AI.method: 本文采用神经架构搜索（NAS）方法，设计更轻量高效的物体跟踪器。
AI.result: 实验表明，LightTrack在性能上优于现有的手工设计的SOTA跟踪器（如SiamRPN++和Ocean），同时使用更少的模型Flops和参数。在资源受限的移动芯片上运行时，LightTrack比Ocean快12倍，参数少13倍，Flops少38倍。
AI.conclusion: LightTrack方法通过NAS设计出的轻量跟踪器在性能和效率上都优于现有方法，有助于缩小学术模型与工业部署之间的差距。

authors_text: Deqing Sun,Daniel Vlasic,Charles Herrmann,Varun Jampani,Michael Krainin,Huiwen Chang,Ramin Zabih,William T. Freeman,Ce Liu
title: AutoFlow: Learning a Better Training Set for Optical Flow
title_zh: AutoFlow：学习更好的光流训练集
abstract: Synthetic datasets play a critical role in pre-training CNN models for optical flow, but they are painstaking to generate and hard to adapt to new applications. To automate the process, we present AutoFlow, a simple and effective method to render training data for optical flow that optimizes the performance of a model on a target dataset. AutoFlow takes a layered approach to render synthetic data, where the motion, shape, and appearance of each layer are controlled by learnable hyperparameters. Experimental results show that AutoFlow achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in pre-training both PWC-Net and RAFT. Our code and data are available at https://autoflow-google.github.io .
summary: Synthetic datasets play a critical role in pre-training CNN models for optical flow, but they are painstaking to generate and hard to adapt to new applications. To automate the process, we present AutoFlow, a simple and effective method to render training data for optical flow that optimizes the performance of a model on a target dataset. AutoFlow takes a layered approach to render synthetic data, where the motion, shape, and appearance of each layer are controlled by learnable hyperparameters. Experimental results show that AutoFlow achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in pre-training both PWC-Net and RAFT. Our code and data are available at https://autoflow-google.github.io .
abstract_zh: 合成数据集在光流 CNN 模型的预训练中发挥着关键作用，但它们的生成非常费力，并且很难适应新的应用。为了实现该过程的自动化，我们提出了 AutoFlow，这是一种简单而有效的方法来渲染光流训练数据，从而优化模型在目标数据集上的性能。 AutoFlow 采用分层方法来渲染合成数据，其中每层的运动、形状和外观均由可学习的超参数控制。实验结果表明，AutoFlow 在预训练 PWC-Net 和 RAFT 方面均达到了最先进的精度。我们的代码和数据可在 https://autoflow-google.github.io 获取。

AI.tldr: AutoFlow提出了一种自动生成光流训练数据的方法，以优化模型在目标数据集上的性能。
AI.motivation: 传统合成数据集生成耗时且难以适配新应用场景，需自动化解决方案提升预训练效率。
AI.method: 采用分层渲染方法，通过可学习的超参数控制各层的运动、形状和外观。
AI.result: 在PWC-Net和RAFT预训练中达到最先进准确性，代码与数据已公开。
AI.conclusion: AutoFlow有效解决了合成数据生成难题，为光流模型预训练提供了高效且通用的解决方案。

authors_text: Jamie Watson,Oisin Mac Aodha,Victor Prisacariu,Gabriel Brostow,Michael Firman
title: The Temporal Opportunist: Self-Supervised Multi-Frame Monocular Depth
title_zh: 时间机会主义者：自监督多帧单目深度
abstract: Self-supervised monocular depth estimation networks are trained to predict scene depth using nearby frames as a supervision signal during training. However, for many applications, sequence information in the form of video frames is also available at test time. The vast majority of monocular networks do not make use of this extra signal, thus ignoring valuable information that could be used to improve the predicted depth. Those that do, either use computationally expensive test-time refinement techniques or off-the-shelf recurrent networks, which only indirectly make use of the geometric information that is inherently available. We propose ManyDepth, an adaptive approach to dense depth estimation that can make use of sequence information at test time, when it is available. Taking inspiration from multi-view stereo, we propose a deep end-to-end cost volume based approach that is trained using self-supervision only. We present a novel consistency loss that encourages the network to ignore the cost volume when it is deemed unreliable, e.g. in the case of moving objects, and an augmentation scheme to cope with static cameras. Our detailed experiments on both KITTI and Cityscapes show that we outperform all published self-supervised baselines, including those that use single or multiple frames at test time.
summary: Self-supervised monocular depth estimation networks are trained to predict scene depth using nearby frames as a supervision signal during training. However, for many applications, sequence information in the form of video frames is also available at test time. The vast majority of monocular networks do not make use of this extra signal, thus ignoring valuable information that could be used to improve the predicted depth. Those that do, either use computationally expensive test-time refinement techniques or off-the-shelf recurrent networks, which only indirectly make use of the geometric information that is inherently available. We propose ManyDepth, an adaptive approach to dense depth estimation that can make use of sequence information at test time, when it is available. Taking inspiration from multi-view stereo, we propose a deep end-to-end cost volume based approach that is trained using self-supervision only. We present a novel consistency loss that encourages the network to ignore the cost volume when it is deemed unreliable, e.g. in the case of moving objects, and an augmentation scheme to cope with static cameras. Our detailed experiments on both KITTI and Cityscapes show that we outperform all published self-supervised baselines, including those that use single or multiple frames at test time.
abstract_zh: 自监督单目深度估计网络经过训练，可以在训练期间使用附近的帧作为监督信号来预测场景深度。然而，对于许多应用来说，视频帧形式的序列信息在测试时也是可用的。绝大多数单目网络不利用这种额外的信号，从而忽略了可用于提高预测深度的有价值的信息。那些这样做的人要么使用计算成本昂贵的测试时间细化技术，要么使用现成的循环网络，这些技术只能间接利用固有可用的几何信息。我们提出了 ManyDepth，一种密集深度估计的自适应方法，可以在测试时利用可用的序列信息。受到多视图立体的启发，我们提出了一种基于深度端到端成本量的方法，该方法仅使用自我监督进行训练。我们提出了一种新颖的一致性损失，鼓励网络在被认为不可靠时忽略成本量，例如在移动物体的情况下，以及应对静态摄像机的增强方案。我们对 KITTI 和 Cityscapes 的详细实验表明，我们的性能优于所有已发布的自我监督基线，包括在测试时使用单帧或多帧的基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自监督多帧单目深度估计方法ManyDepth，通过利用测试时的序列信息提升深度预测效果。
AI.motivation: 现有单目深度估计网络在测试时未有效利用视频序列中的时序信息，导致忽略潜在的深度优化机会。
AI.method: 基于多视角立体视觉思想，设计端到端代价体网络，结合自监督训练与新型一致性损失函数，通过动态忽略不可靠区域（如运动物体）并优化静态相机增强方案。
AI.result: 在KITTI和Cityscapes数据集上超越所有自监督基线方法，包括使用单帧或多帧测试的现有技术。
AI.conclusion: ManyDepth通过有效利用测试时的序列信息，实现了更准确的密集深度估计，为单目深度估计任务提供了新思路。

search_text: The Temporal Opportunist: Self-Supervised Multi-Frame Monocular Depth 时间机会主义者：自监督多帧单目深度 Self-supervised monocular depth estimation networks are trained to predict scene depth using nearby frames as a supervision signal during training. However, for many applications, sequence information in the form of video frames is also available at test time. The vast majority of monocular networks do not make use of this extra signal, thus ignoring valuable information that could be used to improve the predicted depth. Those that do, either use computationally expensive test-time refinement techniques or off-the-shelf recurrent networks, which only indirectly make use of the geometric information that is inherently available. We propose ManyDepth, an adaptive approach to dense depth estimation that can make use of sequence information at test time, when it is available. Taking inspiration from multi-view stereo, we propose a deep end-to-end cost volume based approach that is trained using self-supervision only. We present a novel consistency loss that encourages the network to ignore the cost volume when it is deemed unreliable, e.g. in the case of moving objects, and an augmentation scheme to cope with static cameras. Our detailed experiments on both KITTI and Cityscapes show that we outperform all published self-supervised baselines, including those that use single or multiple frames at test time. 自监督单目深度估计网络经过训练，可以在训练期间使用附近的帧作为监督信号来预测场景深度。然而，对于许多应用来说，视频帧形式的序列信息在测试时也是可用的。绝大多数单目网络不利用这种额外的信号，从而忽略了可用于提高预测深度的有价值的信息。那些这样做的人要么使用计算成本昂贵的测试时间细化技术，要么使用现成的循环网络，这些技术只能间接利用固有可用的几何信息。我们提出了 ManyDepth，一种密集深度估计的自适应方法，可以在测试时利用可用的序列信息。受到多视图立体的启发，我们提出了一种基于深度端到端成本量的方法，该方法仅使用自我监督进行训练。我们提出了一种新颖的一致性损失，鼓励网络在被认为不可靠时忽略成本量，例如在移动物体的情况下，以及应对静态摄像机的增强方案。我们对 KITTI 和 Cityscapes 的详细实验表明，我们的性能优于所有已发布的自我监督基线，包括在测试时使用单帧或多帧的基线。 本文提出一种自监督多帧单目深度估计方法ManyDepth，通过利用测试时的序列信息提升深度预测效果。 Jamie Watson,Oisin Mac Aodha,Victor Prisacariu,Gabriel Brostow,Michael Firman cs.CV

title: A Hierarchical Transformation-Discriminating Generative Model for Few Shot Anomaly Detection
title_zh: 用于少样本异常检测的分层变换判别生成模型
abstract: Anomaly detection, the task of identifying unusual samples in data, often relies on a large set of training samples. In this work, we consider the setting of few-shot anomaly detection in images, where only a few images are given at training. We devise a hierarchical generative model that captures the multi-scale patch distribution of each training image. We further enhance the representation of our model by using image transformations and optimize scale-specific patch-discriminators to distinguish between real and fake patches of the image, as well as between different transformations applied to those patches. The anomaly score is obtained by aggregating the patch-based votes of the correct transformation across scales and image regions. We demonstrate the superiority of our method on both the one-shot and few-shot settings, on the datasets of Paris, CIFAR10, MNIST and FashionMNIST as well as in the setting of defect detection on MVTec. In all cases, our method outperforms the recent baseline methods.
summary: Anomaly detection, the task of identifying unusual samples in data, often relies on a large set of training samples. In this work, we consider the setting of few-shot anomaly detection in images, where only a few images are given at training. We devise a hierarchical generative model that captures the multi-scale patch distribution of each training image. We further enhance the representation of our model by using image transformations and optimize scale-specific patch-discriminators to distinguish between real and fake patches of the image, as well as between different transformations applied to those patches. The anomaly score is obtained by aggregating the patch-based votes of the correct transformation across scales and image regions. We demonstrate the superiority of our method on both the one-shot and few-shot settings, on the datasets of Paris, CIFAR10, MNIST and FashionMNIST as well as in the setting of defect detection on MVTec. In all cases, our method outperforms the recent baseline methods.
abstract_zh: 异常检测是识别数据中异常样本的任务，通常依赖于大量训练样本。在这项工作中，我们考虑图像中少样本异常检测的设置，其中在训练时仅给出少量图像。我们设计了一个分层生成模型来捕获每个训练图像的多尺度补丁分布。我们通过使用图像变换进一步增强模型的表示，并优化特定于比例的补丁鉴别器，以区分图像的真实补丁和假补丁，以及应用于这些补丁的不同变换。异常分数是通过聚合跨尺度和图像区域的正确变换的基于补丁的投票来获得的。我们在 Paris、CIFAR10、MNIST 和 FashionMNIST 数据集以及 MVTec 上的缺陷检测设置上证明了我们的方法在单次和少次设置上的优越性。在所有情况下，我们的方法都优于最近的基线方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层变换判别生成模型，用于解决图像少样本异常检测问题。
AI.motivation: 传统异常检测依赖大量训练样本，但在实际场景中常面临样本稀缺问题，需开发适用于少样本场景的检测方法。
AI.method: 构建分层生成模型捕捉多尺度图像块分布，结合图像变换增强表示，并优化尺度特异性判别器区分真实/伪造块及不同变换方式，通过跨尺度区域的块级投票生成异常分数。
AI.result: 在Paris、CIFAR10、MNIST、FashionMNIST及MVTec缺陷检测数据集上，方法在单样本和少样本设置下均优于最新基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过多尺度变换判别机制有效提升少样本异常检测性能，为数据稀缺场景提供新解决方案。

authors_text: Diego Ferigo,Raffaello Camoriano,Paolo Maria Viceconte,Daniele Calandriello,Silvio Traversaro,Lorenzo Rosasco,Daniele Pucci
title: On the Emergence of Whole-body Strategies from Humanoid Robot Push-recovery Learning
title_zh: 论人形机器人推恢复学习中全身策略的出现
comment: Co-first authors: Diego Ferigo and Raffaello Camoriano; 8 pages
comments: Co-first authors: Diego Ferigo and Raffaello Camoriano; 8 pages
abstract: Balancing and push-recovery are essential capabilities enabling humanoid robots to solve complex locomotion tasks. In this context, classical control systems tend to be based on simplified physical models and hard-coded strategies. Although successful in specific scenarios, this approach requires demanding tuning of parameters and switching logic between specifically-designed controllers for handling more general perturbations. We apply model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning for training a general and robust humanoid push-recovery policy in a simulation environment. Our method targets high-dimensional whole-body humanoid control and is validated on the iCub humanoid. Reward components incorporating expert knowledge on humanoid control enable fast learning of several robust behaviors by the same policy, spanning the entire body. We validate our method with extensive quantitative analyses in simulation, including out-of-sample tasks which demonstrate policy robustness and generalization, both key requirements towards real-world robot deployment.
summary: Balancing and push-recovery are essential capabilities enabling humanoid robots to solve complex locomotion tasks. In this context, classical control systems tend to be based on simplified physical models and hard-coded strategies. Although successful in specific scenarios, this approach requires demanding tuning of parameters and switching logic between specifically-designed controllers for handling more general perturbations. We apply model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning for training a general and robust humanoid push-recovery policy in a simulation environment. Our method targets high-dimensional whole-body humanoid control and is validated on the iCub humanoid. Reward components incorporating expert knowledge on humanoid control enable fast learning of several robust behaviors by the same policy, spanning the entire body. We validate our method with extensive quantitative analyses in simulation, including out-of-sample tasks which demonstrate policy robustness and generalization, both key requirements towards real-world robot deployment.
abstract_zh: 平衡和推动恢复是人形机器人解决复杂运动任务的基本能力。在这种情况下，经典控制系统往往基于简化的物理模型和硬编码策略。尽管在特定场景中取得了成功，但这种方法需要对参数进行严格的调整，并在专门设计的控制器之间切换逻辑，以处理更一般的扰动。我们应用无模型深度强化学习在模拟环境中训练通用且强大的人形推送恢复策略。我们的方法针对高维全身人形控制，并在 iCub 人形机器人上进行了验证。奖励组件结合了人形控制的专业知识，可以通过相同的策略快速学习跨越整个身体的几种鲁棒行为。我们通过模拟中的广泛定量分析来验证我们的方法，包括证明策略稳健性和泛化性的样本外任务，这都是现实世界机器人部署的关键要求。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的人形机器人全身策略学习方法，用于解决复杂环境下的推挤恢复问题。
AI.motivation: 传统控制方法依赖简化模型和硬编码策略，难以应对复杂扰动且参数调优困难，需开发更通用的鲁棒控制策略。
AI.method: 采用无模型深度强化学习，在仿真环境中训练全身控制策略，结合专家知识设计奖励函数，通过iCub人形机器人验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 仿真结果表明策略在多种未见过的任务中表现出良好的鲁棒性和泛化能力，验证了方法在复杂扰动下的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为复杂人形机器人控制提供了灵活的解决方案，通过强化学习实现了全身策略的自主学习，为实际应用奠定基础。

motivation: 传统控制方法依赖简化模型和硬编码策略，难以应对复杂扰动且参数调优困难，需开发更通用的鲁棒控制策略。
method: 采用无模型深度强化学习，在仿真环境中训练全身控制策略，结合专家知识设计奖励函数，通过iCub人形机器人验证方法有效性。
result: 仿真结果表明策略在多种未见过的任务中表现出良好的鲁棒性和泛化能力，验证了方法在复杂扰动下的有效性。
conclusion: 该方法为复杂人形机器人控制提供了灵活的解决方案，通过强化学习实现了全身策略的自主学习，为实际应用奠定基础。
search_text: On the Emergence of Whole-body Strategies from Humanoid Robot Push-recovery Learning 论人形机器人推恢复学习中全身策略的出现 Balancing and push-recovery are essential capabilities enabling humanoid robots to solve complex locomotion tasks. In this context, classical control systems tend to be based on simplified physical models and hard-coded strategies. Although successful in specific scenarios, this approach requires demanding tuning of parameters and switching logic between specifically-designed controllers for handling more general perturbations. We apply model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning for training a general and robust humanoid push-recovery policy in a simulation environment. Our method targets high-dimensional whole-body humanoid control and is validated on the iCub humanoid. Reward components incorporating expert knowledge on humanoid control enable fast learning of several robust behaviors by the same policy, spanning the entire body. We validate our method with extensive quantitative analyses in simulation, including out-of-sample tasks which demonstrate policy robustness and generalization, both key requirements towards real-world robot deployment. 平衡和推动恢复是人形机器人解决复杂运动任务的基本能力。在这种情况下，经典控制系统往往基于简化的物理模型和硬编码策略。尽管在特定场景中取得了成功，但这种方法需要对参数进行严格的调整，并在专门设计的控制器之间切换逻辑，以处理更一般的扰动。我们应用无模型深度强化学习在模拟环境中训练通用且强大的人形推送恢复策略。我们的方法针对高维全身人形控制，并在 iCub 人形机器人上进行了验证。奖励组件结合了人形控制的专业知识，可以通过相同的策略快速学习跨越整个身体的几种鲁棒行为。我们通过模拟中的广泛定量分析来验证我们的方法，包括证明策略稳健性和泛化性的样本外任务，这都是现实世界机器人部署的关键要求。 本文提出了一种基于深度强化学习的人形机器人全身策略学习方法，用于解决复杂环境下的推挤恢复问题。 Diego Ferigo,Raffaello Camoriano,Paolo Maria Viceconte,Daniele Calandriello,Silvio Traversaro,Lorenzo Rosasco,Daniele Pucci cs.RO,cs.LG,stat.ML

authors_text: Haoyuan Chen,Chen Li,Ge Wang,Xiaoyan Li,Md Rahaman,Hongzan Sun,Weiming Hu,Yixin Li,Wanli Liu,Changhao Sun,Shiliang Ai,Marcin Grzegorzek
title: GasHis-Transformer: A Multi-scale Visual Transformer Approach for Gastric Histopathological Image Detection
title_zh: GasHis-Transformer：用于胃组织病理学图像检测的多尺度视觉变压器方法
abstract: In this paper, a multi-scale visual transformer model, referred as GasHis-Transformer, is proposed for Gastric Histopathological Image Detection (GHID), which enables the automatic global detection of gastric cancer images. GasHis-Transformer model consists of two key modules designed to extract global and local information using a position-encoded transformer model and a convolutional neural network with local convolution, respectively. A publicly available hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stained gastric histopathological image dataset is used in the experiment. Furthermore, a Dropconnect based lightweight network is proposed to reduce the model size and training time of GasHis-Transformer for clinical applications with improved confidence. Moreover, a series of contrast and extended experiments verify the robustness, extensibility and stability of GasHis-Transformer. In conclusion, GasHis-Transformer demonstrates high global detection performance and shows its significant potential in GHID task.
summary: In this paper, a multi-scale visual transformer model, referred as GasHis-Transformer, is proposed for Gastric Histopathological Image Detection (GHID), which enables the automatic global detection of gastric cancer images. GasHis-Transformer model consists of two key modules designed to extract global and local information using a position-encoded transformer model and a convolutional neural network with local convolution, respectively. A publicly available hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stained gastric histopathological image dataset is used in the experiment. Furthermore, a Dropconnect based lightweight network is proposed to reduce the model size and training time of GasHis-Transformer for clinical applications with improved confidence. Moreover, a series of contrast and extended experiments verify the robustness, extensibility and stability of GasHis-Transformer. In conclusion, GasHis-Transformer demonstrates high global detection performance and shows its significant potential in GHID task.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种用于胃组织病理学图像检测（GHID）的多尺度视觉变压器模型，称为GasHis-Transformer，它能够实现胃癌图像的自动全局检测。 GasHis-Transformer 模型由两个关键模块组成，分别使用位置编码变压器模型和具有局部卷积的卷积神经网络来提取全局和局部信息。实验中使用公开的苏木精和伊红 (H&E) 染色的胃组织病理学图像数据集。此外，还提出了基于 Dropconnect 的轻量级网络，以减少 GasHis-Transformer 的模型大小和训练时间，以提高临床应用的置信度。此外，一系列对比和扩展实验验证了GasHis-Transformer的鲁棒性、可扩展性和稳定性。总之，GasHis-Transformer 展示了较高的全局检测性能，并显示了其在 GHID 任务中的巨大潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多尺度视觉Transformer模型GasHis-Transformer，用于胃癌组织病理图像的全局自动检测。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在胃癌组织病理图像检测中存在全局特征提取不足、模型复杂度高导致临床应用受限的问题。
AI.method: 融合位置编码Transformer（全局特征提取）与局部卷积神经网络（局部特征提取），并设计基于Dropconnect的轻量化网络以降低模型规模和训练时间。
AI.result: 实验验证了模型在公开H&E数据集上的鲁棒性、可扩展性及稳定性，取得高精度全局检测性能。
AI.conclusion: GasHis-Transformer在胃癌组织病理图像检测任务中展现出显著潜力，适用于临床场景。

title: Online certification of preference-based fairness for personalized recommender systems
title_zh: 个性化推荐系统基于偏好的公平性在线认证
abstract: Recommender systems are facing scrutiny because of their growing impact on the opportunities we have access to. Current audits for fairness are limited to coarse-grained parity assessments at the level of sensitive groups. We propose to audit for envy-freeness, a more granular criterion aligned with individual preferences: every user should prefer their recommendations to those of other users. Since auditing for envy requires to estimate the preferences of users beyond their existing recommendations, we cast the audit as a new pure exploration problem in multi-armed bandits. We propose a sample-efficient algorithm with theoretical guarantees that it does not deteriorate user experience. We also study the trade-offs achieved on real-world recommendation datasets.
summary: Recommender systems are facing scrutiny because of their growing impact on the opportunities we have access to. Current audits for fairness are limited to coarse-grained parity assessments at the level of sensitive groups. We propose to audit for envy-freeness, a more granular criterion aligned with individual preferences: every user should prefer their recommendations to those of other users. Since auditing for envy requires to estimate the preferences of users beyond their existing recommendations, we cast the audit as a new pure exploration problem in multi-armed bandits. We propose a sample-efficient algorithm with theoretical guarantees that it does not deteriorate user experience. We also study the trade-offs achieved on real-world recommendation datasets.
abstract_zh: 推荐系统正面临审查，因为它们对我们获得的机会的影响越来越大。目前的公平性审计仅限于敏感群体层面的粗粒度平等评估。我们建议审核无嫉妒性，这是一个与个人偏好一致的更细粒度的标准：每个用户都应该更喜欢自己的推荐而不是其他用户的推荐。由于嫉妒审计需要估计用户的偏好，超出他们现有的建议，因此我们将审计视为多臂老虎机中的一个新的纯粹探索问题。我们提出了一种样本有效的算法，理论上保证它不会恶化用户体验。我们还研究了在现实世界的推荐数据集上实现的权衡。

AI.tldr: 提出一种在线认证方法，通过基于偏好的公平性审计（无嫉妒标准）来评估个性化推荐系统，确保用户更偏好自己的推荐。
AI.motivation: 现有公平性审计方法局限于敏感群体的粗粒度评估，无法满足个性化推荐中用户个体偏好差异的需求，需更细致的公平性标准。
AI.method: 将公平性审计建模为多臂老虎机的纯探索问题，设计样本高效的算法，在保证理论性能的同时不降低用户体验。
AI.result: 在真实推荐数据集上验证了算法的有效性，分析了公平性与推荐质量之间的权衡。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法实现了基于个体偏好的公平性审计，在保证用户体验的前提下提升推荐系统的公平性。

title: Brain-inspired computing: We need a master plan
title_zh: 类脑计算：我们需要一个总体规划
abstract: New computing technologies inspired by the brain promise fundamentally different ways to process information with extreme energy efficiency and the ability to handle the avalanche of unstructured and noisy data that we are generating at an ever-increasing rate. To realise this promise requires a brave and coordinated plan to bring together disparate research communities and to provide them with the funding, focus and support needed. We have done this in the past with digital technologies; we are in the process of doing it with quantum technologies; can we now do it for brain-inspired computing?
summary: New computing technologies inspired by the brain promise fundamentally different ways to process information with extreme energy efficiency and the ability to handle the avalanche of unstructured and noisy data that we are generating at an ever-increasing rate. To realise this promise requires a brave and coordinated plan to bring together disparate research communities and to provide them with the funding, focus and support needed. We have done this in the past with digital technologies; we are in the process of doing it with quantum technologies; can we now do it for brain-inspired computing?
abstract_zh: 受大脑启发的新计算技术有望以根本不同的方式处理信息，并具有极高的能源效率，并能够处理我们以不断增长的速度生成的大量非结构化和噪声数据。为了实现这一承诺，需要一个勇敢而协调的计划，将不同的研究团体聚集在一起，并为他们提供所需的资金、重点和支持。我们过去已经通过数字技术做到了这一点；我们正在利用量子技术来做到这一点；我们现在可以为类脑计算做到这一点吗？

AI.tldr: 呼吁制定全面计划以推动脑启发计算的发展，整合不同研究领域并提供必要资源支持。
AI.motivation: 应对数据激增挑战，传统计算方式不足，脑启发计算需整合资源与社区以实现潜力。
AI.method: 通过类比历史技术发展，提出整合研究社区、提供资金支持的协调计划。
AI.result: 提出制定协调计划的必要性，呼吁跨领域合作与资源投入。
AI.conclusion: 制定类似数字与量子技术的协调计划，可推动脑启发计算实现高效数据处理潜力。

search_text: Brain-inspired computing: We need a master plan 类脑计算：我们需要一个总体规划 New computing technologies inspired by the brain promise fundamentally different ways to process information with extreme energy efficiency and the ability to handle the avalanche of unstructured and noisy data that we are generating at an ever-increasing rate. To realise this promise requires a brave and coordinated plan to bring together disparate research communities and to provide them with the funding, focus and support needed. We have done this in the past with digital technologies; we are in the process of doing it with quantum technologies; can we now do it for brain-inspired computing? 受大脑启发的新计算技术有望以根本不同的方式处理信息，并具有极高的能源效率，并能够处理我们以不断增长的速度生成的大量非结构化和噪声数据。为了实现这一承诺，需要一个勇敢而协调的计划，将不同的研究团体聚集在一起，并为他们提供所需的资金、重点和支持。我们过去已经通过数字技术做到了这一点；我们正在利用量子技术来做到这一点；我们现在可以为类脑计算做到这一点吗？ 呼吁制定全面计划以推动脑启发计算的发展，整合不同研究领域并提供必要资源支持。 Adnan Mehonic,Anthony J Kenyon cs.ET,cs.AI,eess.IV

title: A General Katsuno-Mendelzon-Style Characterization of AGM Belief Base Revision for Arbitrary Monotonic Logics
title_zh: 任意单调逻辑的 AGM 信念基修正的一般 Katsuno-Mendelzon 式表征
comment: This article constitutes a preliminary report, which was found to contain inaccuracies. It is superseded by a significantly generalized, extended, and revised treatise by the same authors made available on arXiv.org under the title "Semantic Characterizations of General Belief Base Revision" on the 27th of December 2021 via arXiv:2112.13557
comments: This article constitutes a preliminary report, which was found to contain inaccuracies. It is superseded by a significantly generalized, extended, and revised treatise by the same authors made available on arXiv.org under the title "Semantic Characterizations of General Belief Base Revision" on the 27th of December 2021 via arXiv:2112.13557
abstract: The AGM postulates by Alchourrón, Gärdenfors, and Makinson continue to represent a cornerstone in research related to belief change. We generalize the approach of Katsuno and Mendelzon (KM) for characterizing AGM base revision from propositional logic to the setting of (multiple) base revision in arbitrary monotonic logics. Our core result is a representation theorem using the assignment of total - yet not transitive - "preference" relations to belief bases. We also provide a characterization of all logics for which our result can be strengthened to preorder assignments (as in KM's original work).
summary: The AGM postulates by Alchourrón, Gärdenfors, and Makinson continue to represent a cornerstone in research related to belief change. We generalize the approach of Katsuno and Mendelzon (KM) for characterizing AGM base revision from propositional logic to the setting of (multiple) base revision in arbitrary monotonic logics. Our core result is a representation theorem using the assignment of total - yet not transitive - "preference" relations to belief bases. We also provide a characterization of all logics for which our result can be strengthened to preorder assignments (as in KM's original work).
abstract_zh: Alchourrón、Gärdenfors 和 Makinson 提出的年度股东大会假设继续代表着信念改变相关研究的基石。我们将 Katsuno 和 Mendelzon (KM) 的方法概括为将 AGM 基础修订从命题逻辑描述为任意单调逻辑中的（多个）基础修订的设置。我们的核心结果是一个表示定理，该定理使用了将总的（但不是传递性的）“偏好”关系分配给信念基础。我们还提供了所有逻辑的特征，我们的结果可以加强为预排序任务（如 KM 的原始工作）。

AI.tldr: 本文将Katsuno-Mendelzon风格的AGM信念基修订理论从命题逻辑推广到任意单调逻辑中的多基修订场景。
AI.motivation: AGM公理体系是信念变更研究的核心框架，但现有Katsuno-Mendelzon方法仅适用于命题逻辑，缺乏对更广泛单调逻辑的普适性刻画。
AI.method: 通过为信念基分配非传递的全序偏好关系构建表示定理，并分析哪些逻辑可强化为预序分配（即KM原始工作中的条件）。
AI.result: 提出适用于任意单调逻辑的信念基修订表示定理，并明确界定可升级为预序分配的逻辑类型。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为不同单调逻辑下的信念基修订提供了统一框架，扩展了KM理论的适用边界并揭示了预序分配的逻辑前提。

title: Improved Kernels for Edge Modification Problems
title_zh: 针对边缘修改问题的改进内核
abstract: In an edge modification problem, we are asked to modify at most $k$ edges to a given graph to make the graph satisfy a certain property. Depending on the operations allowed, we have the completion problems and the edge deletion problems. A great amount of efforts have been devoted to understanding the kernelization complexity of these problems. We revisit several well-studied edge modification problems, and develop improved kernels for them: \begin{itemize} \item a $2 k$-vertex kernel for the cluster edge deletion problem, \item a $3 k^2$-vertex kernel for the trivially perfect completion problem, \item a $5 k^{1.5}$-vertex kernel for the split completion problem and the split edge deletion problem, and \item a $5 k^{1.5}$-vertex kernel for the pseudo-split completion problem and the pseudo-split edge deletion problem. \end{itemize} Moreover, our kernels for split completion and pseudo-split completion have only $O(k^{2.5})$ edges. Our results also include a $2 k$-vertex kernel for the strong triadic closure problem, which is related to cluster edge deletion.
summary: In an edge modification problem, we are asked to modify at most $k$ edges to a given graph to make the graph satisfy a certain property. Depending on the operations allowed, we have the completion problems and the edge deletion problems. A great amount of efforts have been devoted to understanding the kernelization complexity of these problems. We revisit several well-studied edge modification problems, and develop improved kernels for them: \begin{itemize} \item a $2 k$-vertex kernel for the cluster edge deletion problem, \item a $3 k^2$-vertex kernel for the trivially perfect completion problem, \item a $5 k^{1.5}$-vertex kernel for the split completion problem and the split edge deletion problem, and \item a $5 k^{1.5}$-vertex kernel for the pseudo-split completion problem and the pseudo-split edge deletion problem. \end{itemize} Moreover, our kernels for split completion and pseudo-split completion have only $O(k^{2.5})$ edges. Our results also include a $2 k$-vertex kernel for the strong triadic closure problem, which is related to cluster edge deletion.
abstract_zh: 在边修改问题中，我们被要求修改给定图的最多$k$条边，以使图满足某个属性。根据允许的操作，我们有完成问题和边缘删除问题。为了理解这些问题的内核化复杂性，人们付出了大量的努力。我们重新审视了几个经过充分研究的边缘修改问题，并为它们开发了改进的内核：\begin{itemize} \item a $2 k$-顶点内核用于簇边删除问题，\item a $3 k^2$-顶点内核用于平凡完美完成问题，\item a $5 k^{1.5}$-顶点内核用于分割完成问题和分割边缘删除问题，以及 \item a $5 k^{1.5}$-顶点内核用于伪分裂完成问题和伪分裂边删除问题。 \end{itemize} 此外，我们用于分割完成和伪分割完成的内核只有 $O(k^{2.5})$ 边。我们的结果还包括用于强三元闭包问题的 2 k$ 顶点内核，该问题与簇边缘删除相关。

AI.tldr: 本文针对边修改问题提出改进的核方法，通过减少图的顶点和边数量来优化问题求解效率。
AI.motivation: 边修改问题在图论中具有重要应用，但现有核方法的效率和规模仍有提升空间，需进一步优化参数化复杂度。
AI.method: 通过重新分析经典边修改问题的结构特性，设计针对性的顶点和边缩减规则，结合参数化复杂度理论改进核的构造方法。
AI.result: 为多个边修改问题提供了更优的核规模，包括2k顶点核、3k²顶点核及5k¹·⁵顶点核，并首次实现split和pseudo-split问题的O(k²·⁵)边核。
AI.conclusion: 改进的核方法显著提升了边修改问题的求解效率，为参数化算法设计提供了新思路，同时验证了经典问题的进一步优化潜力。

title: Explainable AI For COVID-19 CT Classifiers: An Initial Comparison Study
title_zh: 适用于 COVID-19 CT 分类器的可解释 AI：初步比较研究
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has made leapfrogs in development across all the industrial sectors especially when deep learning has been introduced. Deep learning helps to learn the behaviour of an entity through methods of recognising and interpreting patterns. Despite its limitless potential, the mystery is how deep learning algorithms make a decision in the first place. Explainable AI (XAI) is the key to unlocking AI and the black-box for deep learning. XAI is an AI model that is programmed to explain its goals, logic, and decision making so that the end users can understand. The end users can be domain experts, regulatory agencies, managers and executive board members, data scientists, users that use AI, with or without awareness, or someone who is affected by the decisions of an AI model. Chest CT has emerged as a valuable tool for the clinical diagnostic and treatment management of the lung diseases associated with COVID-19. AI can support rapid evaluation of CT scans to differentiate COVID-19 findings from other lung diseases. However, how these AI tools or deep learning algorithms reach such a decision and which are the most influential features derived from these neural networks with typically deep layers are not clear. The aim of this study is to propose and develop XAI strategies for COVID-19 classification models with an investigation of comparison. The results demonstrate promising quantification and qualitative visualisations that can further enhance the clinician's understanding and decision making with more granular information from the results given by the learned XAI models.

The aim of this study is to propose and develop XAI strategies for COVID-19 classification models with an investigation of comparison. The results demonstrate promising quantification and qualitative visualisations that can further enhance the clinician's understanding and decision making with more granular information from the results given by the learned XAI models.
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）在所有工业领域都取得了跨越式发展，尤其是深度学习的引入。深度学习有助于通过识别和解释模式的方法来学习实体的行为。尽管其潜力无限，但深度学习算法最初是如何做出决定的却是个谜。可解释的人工智能（XAI）是解锁人工智能和深度学习黑匣子的钥匙。 XAI 是一种人工智能模型，经过编程可以解释其目标、逻辑和决策，以便最终用户能够理解。最终用户可以是领域专家、监管机构、经理和执行委员会成员、数据科学家、使用人工智能的用户（无论是否有意识）或受人工智能模型决策影响的人。胸部 CT 已成为与 COVID-19 相关的肺部疾病的临床诊断和治疗管理的重要工具。 AI 可以支持 CT 扫描的快速评估，以区分 COVID-19 的发现与其他肺部疾病。然而，这些人工智能工具或深度学习算法如何做出这样的决定，以及从这些具有典型深层的神经网络中衍生出哪些最有影响力的特征尚不清楚。本研究的目的是通过比较研究提出和开发用于 COVID-19 分类模型的 XAI 策略。结果表明，定量和定性可视化很有前景，可以通过学习的 XAI 模型给出的结果中提供更精细的信息，进一步增强临床医生的理解和决策。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在为COVID-19 CT分类模型开发并比较可解释AI（XAI）策略，以增强模型决策的透明度。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型在医学诊断中广泛应用，但其决策过程缺乏透明度，阻碍了临床信任与应用。需通过XAI技术揭示模型关键特征，辅助医生理解诊断依据。
AI.method: 研究采用多种XAI方法（如特征可视化、归因分析）对COVID-19 CT分类模型进行解释，并通过定量与定性分析比较不同方法的效果。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，XAI技术能有效量化关键特征并生成可视化解释，帮助临床医生更细致地理解模型决策，提升诊断可信度。
AI.conclusion: XAI策略可显著增强AI在COVID-19 CT诊断中的可解释性，促进医患信任及模型在临床场景中的实际应用。

search_text: Explainable AI For COVID-19 CT Classifiers: An Initial Comparison Study 适用于 COVID-19 CT 分类器的可解释 AI：初步比较研究 Artificial Intelligence (AI) has made leapfrogs in development across all the industrial sectors especially when deep learning has been introduced. Deep learning helps to learn the behaviour of an entity through methods of recognising and interpreting patterns. Despite its limitless potential, the mystery is how deep learning algorithms make a decision in the first place. Explainable AI (XAI) is the key to unlocking AI and the black-box for deep learning. XAI is an AI model that is programmed to explain its goals, logic, and decision making so that the end users can understand. The end users can be domain experts, regulatory agencies, managers and executive board members, data scientists, users that use AI, with or without awareness, or someone who is affected by the decisions of an AI model. Chest CT has emerged as a valuable tool for the clinical diagnostic and treatment management of the lung diseases associated with COVID-19. AI can support rapid evaluation of CT scans to differentiate COVID-19 findings from other lung diseases. However, how these AI tools or deep learning algorithms reach such a decision and which are the most influential features derived from these neural networks with typically deep layers are not clear. The aim of this study is to propose and develop XAI strategies for COVID-19 classification models with an investigation of comparison. The results demonstrate promising quantification and qualitative visualisations that can further enhance the clinician's understanding and decision making with more granular information from the results given by the learned XAI models. 人工智能（AI）在所有工业领域都取得了跨越式发展，尤其是深度学习的引入。深度学习有助于通过识别和解释模式的方法来学习实体的行为。尽管其潜力无限，但深度学习算法最初是如何做出决定的却是个谜。可解释的人工智能（XAI）是解锁人工智能和深度学习黑匣子的钥匙。 XAI 是一种人工智能模型，经过编程可以解释其目标、逻辑和决策，以便最终用户能够理解。最终用户可以是领域专家、监管机构、经理和执行委员会成员、数据科学家、使用人工智能的用户（无论是否有意识）或受人工智能模型决策影响的人。胸部 CT 已成为与 COVID-19 相关的肺部疾病的临床诊断和治疗管理的重要工具。 AI 可以支持 CT 扫描的快速评估，以区分 COVID-19 的发现与其他肺部疾病。然而，这些人工智能工具或深度学习算法如何做出这样的决定，以及从这些具有典型深层的神经网络中衍生出哪些最有影响力的特征尚不清楚。本研究的目的是通过比较研究提出和开发用于 COVID-19 分类模型的 XAI 策略。结果表明，定量和定性可视化很有前景，可以通过学习的 XAI 模型给出的结果中提供更精细的信息，进一步增强临床医生的理解和决策。 该论文旨在为COVID-19 CT分类模型开发并比较可解释AI（XAI）策略，以增强模型决策的透明度。 Qinghao Ye,Jun Xia,Guang Yang cs.LG,cs.CV,eess.IV

authors_text: Markus Freitag,George Foster,David Grangier,Viresh Ratnakar,Qijun Tan,Wolfgang Macherey
title: Experts, Errors, and Context: A Large-Scale Study of Human Evaluation for Machine Translation
title_zh: 专家、错误和背景：机器翻译人类评价的大规模研究
abstract: Human evaluation of modern high-quality machine translation systems is a difficult problem, and there is increasing evidence that inadequate evaluation procedures can lead to erroneous conclusions. While there has been considerable research on human evaluation, the field still lacks a commonly-accepted standard procedure. As a step toward this goal, we propose an evaluation methodology grounded in explicit error analysis, based on the Multidimensional Quality Metrics (MQM) framework. We carry out the largest MQM research study to date, scoring the outputs of top systems from the WMT 2020 shared task in two language pairs using annotations provided by professional translators with access to full document context. We analyze the resulting data extensively, finding among other results a substantially different ranking of evaluated systems from the one established by the WMT crowd workers, exhibiting a clear preference for human over machine output. Surprisingly, we also find that automatic metrics based on pre-trained embeddings can outperform human crowd workers. We make our corpus publicly available for further research.
summary: Human evaluation of modern high-quality machine translation systems is a difficult problem, and there is increasing evidence that inadequate evaluation procedures can lead to erroneous conclusions. While there has been considerable research on human evaluation, the field still lacks a commonly-accepted standard procedure. As a step toward this goal, we propose an evaluation methodology grounded in explicit error analysis, based on the Multidimensional Quality Metrics (MQM) framework. We carry out the largest MQM research study to date, scoring the outputs of top systems from the WMT 2020 shared task in two language pairs using annotations provided by professional translators with access to full document context. We analyze the resulting data extensively, finding among other results a substantially different ranking of evaluated systems from the one established by the WMT crowd workers, exhibiting a clear preference for human over machine output. Surprisingly, we also find that automatic metrics based on pre-trained embeddings can outperform human crowd workers. We make our corpus publicly available for further research.
abstract_zh: 对现代高质量机器翻译系统进行人工评估是一个难题，并且越来越多的证据表明，不充分的评估程序可能会导致错误的结论。尽管对人类评估进行了大量研究，但该领域仍然缺乏普遍接受的标准程序。作为实现这一目标的一步，我们提出了一种基于多维质量指标 (MQM) 框架、基于显式误差分析的评估方法。我们开展了迄今为止规模最大的 MQM 研究，使用专业翻译人员提供的可访问完整文档上下文的注释，对 WMT 2020 共享任务的顶级系统的输出以两种语言对进行评分。我们对所得数据进行了广泛分析，发现评估系统的排名与 WMT 众包工作者建立的排名有很大不同，显示出对人类产出的明显偏好。令人惊讶的是，我们还发现基于预先训练的嵌入的自动指标可以优于人类人群工作人员。我们公开我们的语料库以供进一步研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于显式错误分析的机器翻译系统评估方法，通过专业译员的多维质量指标（MQM）评分，对比现有评估方式并验证自动指标的有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有机器翻译评估方法存在流程不统一、易产生错误结论的问题，缺乏公认的标准化评估体系。
AI.method: 基于MQM框架，采用专业译员对WMT 2020任务中的翻译系统输出进行全文档上下文评分，并对比众包评估与预训练嵌入自动指标的表现。
AI.result: 发现系统排名与WMT众包评估结果显著不同，译员更偏好人类输出；意外发现基于预训练嵌入的自动指标可优于众包评估。
AI.conclusion: 提出的新评估方法为行业提供标准化参考，验证了自动指标的潜力，并公开数据集推动后续研究。

title: Reverse Shortest Path Problem for Unit-Disk Graphs
title_zh: 单位圆盘图的逆向最短路径问题
comment: This version added new results on the weighted case and the L1 case. These new results have been accepted in WALCOM 2022 and have also been presented at FWCG 2021
comments: This version added new results on the weighted case and the L1 case. These new results have been accepted in WALCOM 2022 and have also been presented at FWCG 2021
abstract: Given a set P of n points in the plane, the unit-disk graph G_{r}(P) with respect to a parameter r is an undirected graph whose vertex set is P such that an edge connects two points p, q \in P if the Euclidean distance between p and q is at most r (the weight of the edge is 1 in the unweighted case and is the distance between p and q in the weighted case). Given a value λ>0 and two points s and t of P, we consider the following reverse shortest path problem: computing the smallest r such that the shortest path length between s and t in G_r(P) is at most λ. In this paper, we present an algorithm of O(\lfloor λ\rfloor \cdot n \log n) time and another algorithm of O(n^{5/4} \log^{7/4} n) time for the unweighted case, as well as an O(n^{5/4} \log^{5/2} n) time algorithm for the weighted case. We also consider the L_1 version of the problem where the distance of two points is measured by the L_1 metric; we solve the problem in O(n \log^3 n) time for both the unweighted and weighted cases.
summary: Given a set P of n points in the plane, the unit-disk graph G_{r}(P) with respect to a parameter r is an undirected graph whose vertex set is P such that an edge connects two points p, q \in P if the Euclidean distance between p and q is at most r (the weight of the edge is 1 in the unweighted case and is the distance between p and q in the weighted case). Given a value λ>0 and two points s and t of P, we consider the following reverse shortest path problem: computing the smallest r such that the shortest path length between s and t in G_r(P) is at most λ. In this paper, we present an algorithm of O(\lfloor λ\rfloor \cdot n \log n) time and another algorithm of O(n^{5/4} \log^{7/4} n) time for the unweighted case, as well as an O(n^{5/4} \log^{5/2} n) time algorithm for the weighted case. We also consider the L_1 version of the problem where the distance of two points is measured by the L_1 metric; we solve the problem in O(n \log^3 n) time for both the unweighted and weighted cases.
abstract_zh: 给定平面上 n 个点的集合 P，关于参数 r 的单位圆盘图 G_{r}(P) 是一个无向图，其顶点集为 P，使得一条边连接两个点 p, q \in P，如果 p 和 q 之间的欧几里德距离至多为 r（在未加权情况下边的权重为 1，在加权情况下边的权重为 p 和 q 之间的距离）。给定值 λ>0 以及 P 的两个点 s 和 t，我们考虑以下反向最短路径问题：计算最小的 r，使得 G_r(P) 中 s 和 t 之间的最短路径长度至多为 λ。在本文中，我们针对未加权情况提出了一种 O(\lfloor λ\rfloor \cdot n \log n) 时间的算法和另一种 O(n^{5/4} \log^{7/4} n) 时间的算法，以及针对加权情况的 O(n^{5/4} \log^{5/2} n) 时间的算法。我们还考虑问题的 L_1 版本，其中两点的距离由 L_1 度量来测量；对于未加权和加权的情况，我们都在 O(n \log^3 n) 时间内解决了问题。

AI.tldr: 本文研究单位圆盘图中的反向最短路径问题，旨在找到最小的半径r，使得两点间的最短路径长度不超过给定值λ。
AI.motivation: 单位圆盘图在无线网络和传感器网络中广泛应用，但现有方法难以高效解决反向最短路径问题，需设计更优算法。
AI.method: 提出多种算法，包括基于分治策略的O(⌊λ⌋·n log n)时间算法，以及针对无权和有权情况的近似算法，并考虑L1度量下的优化方法。
AI.result: 为无权情况设计O(n^{5/4} log^{7/4}n)时间算法，有权情况为O(n^{5/4} log^{5/2}n)，L1度量下均达到O(n log^3 n)时间复杂度。
AI.conclusion: 通过不同算法有效解决反向最短路径问题，尤其在L1度量下实现高效计算，为相关应用提供理论支持。

search_text: Reverse Shortest Path Problem for Unit-Disk Graphs 单位圆盘图的逆向最短路径问题 Given a set P of n points in the plane, the unit-disk graph G_{r}(P) with respect to a parameter r is an undirected graph whose vertex set is P such that an edge connects two points p, q \in P if the Euclidean distance between p and q is at most r (the weight of the edge is 1 in the unweighted case and is the distance between p and q in the weighted case). Given a value λ>0 and two points s and t of P, we consider the following reverse shortest path problem: computing the smallest r such that the shortest path length between s and t in G_r(P) is at most λ. In this paper, we present an algorithm of O(\lfloor λ\rfloor \cdot n \log n) time and another algorithm of O(n^{5/4} \log^{7/4} n) time for the unweighted case, as well as an O(n^{5/4} \log^{5/2} n) time algorithm for the weighted case. We also consider the L_1 version of the problem where the distance of two points is measured by the L_1 metric; we solve the problem in O(n \log^3 n) time for both the unweighted and weighted cases. 给定平面上 n 个点的集合 P，关于参数 r 的单位圆盘图 G_{r}(P) 是一个无向图，其顶点集为 P，使得一条边连接两个点 p, q \in P，如果 p 和 q 之间的欧几里德距离至多为 r（在未加权情况下边的权重为 1，在加权情况下边的权重为 p 和 q 之间的距离）。给定值 λ>0 以及 P 的两个点 s 和 t，我们考虑以下反向最短路径问题：计算最小的 r，使得 G_r(P) 中 s 和 t 之间的最短路径长度至多为 λ。在本文中，我们针对未加权情况提出了一种 O(\lfloor λ\rfloor \cdot n \log n) 时间的算法和另一种 O(n^{5/4} \log^{7/4} n) 时间的算法，以及针对加权情况的 O(n^{5/4} \log^{5/2} n) 时间的算法。我们还考虑问题的 L_1 版本，其中两点的距离由 L_1 度量来测量；对于未加权和加权的情况，我们都在 O(n \log^3 n) 时间内解决了问题。 本文研究单位圆盘图中的反向最短路径问题，旨在找到最小的半径r，使得两点间的最短路径长度不超过给定值λ。 Haitao Wang,Yiming Zhao cs.CG,cs.DS

title: Impact of Encoding and Segmentation Strategies on End-to-End Simultaneous Speech Translation
title_zh: 编码和分段策略对端到端同步语音翻译的影响
abstract: Boosted by the simultaneous translation shared task at IWSLT 2020, promising end-to-end online speech translation approaches were recently proposed. They consist in incrementally encoding a speech input (in a source language) and decoding the corresponding text (in a target language) with the best possible trade-off between latency and translation quality. This paper investigates two key aspects of end-to-end simultaneous speech translation: (a) how to encode efficiently the continuous speech flow, and (b) how to segment the speech flow in order to alternate optimally between reading (R: encoding input) and writing (W: decoding output) operations. We extend our previously proposed end-to-end online decoding strategy and show that while replacing BLSTM by ULSTM encoding degrades performance in offline mode, it actually improves both efficiency and performance in online mode. We also measure the impact of different methods to segment the speech signal (using fixed interval boundaries, oracle word boundaries or randomly set boundaries) and show that our best end-to-end online decoding strategy is surprisingly the one that alternates R/W operations on fixed size blocks on our English-German speech translation setup.
summary: Boosted by the simultaneous translation shared task at IWSLT 2020, promising end-to-end online speech translation approaches were recently proposed. They consist in incrementally encoding a speech input (in a source language) and decoding the corresponding text (in a target language) with the best possible trade-off between latency and translation quality. This paper investigates two key aspects of end-to-end simultaneous speech translation: (a) how to encode efficiently the continuous speech flow, and (b) how to segment the speech flow in order to alternate optimally between reading (R: encoding input) and writing (W: decoding output) operations. We extend our previously proposed end-to-end online decoding strategy and show that while replacing BLSTM by ULSTM encoding degrades performance in offline mode, it actually improves both efficiency and performance in online mode. We also measure the impact of different methods to segment the speech signal (using fixed interval boundaries, oracle word boundaries or randomly set boundaries) and show that our best end-to-end online decoding strategy is surprisingly the one that alternates R/W operations on fixed size blocks on our English-German speech translation setup.
abstract_zh: 在 IWSLT 2020 的同声翻译共享任务的推动下，最近提出了有前途的端到端在线语音翻译方法。它们包括增量编码语音输入（以源语言）并解码相应的文本（以目标语言），并在延迟和翻译质量之间实现最佳权衡。本文研究了端到端同步语音翻译的两个关键方面：（a）如何有效地编码连续语音流，以及（b）如何对语音流进行分段，以便在读取（R：编码输入）和写入（W：解码输出）操作之间进行最佳交替。我们扩展了之前提出的端到端在线解码策略，并表明虽然用 ULSTM 编码替换 BLSTM 会降低离线模式下的性能，但它实际上提高了在线模式下的效率和性能。我们还测量了不同方法对语音信号进行分段（使用固定间隔边界、oracle 字边界或随机设置边界）的影响，并表明我们最好的端到端在线解码策略令人惊讶的是在我们的英语-德语语音翻译设置中交替对固定大小块进行 R/W 操作。

AI.tldr: 研究编码与分段策略对端到端实时语音翻译的影响，探索如何优化语音流的编码效率和读写操作切换方式。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端实时语音翻译需在延迟与翻译质量间权衡，需优化编码与分段策略以提升效率与效果。
AI.method: 扩展在线解码策略，对比BLSTM与ULSTM编码效果，评估固定间隔、oracle词边界及随机边界等分段方法。
AI.result: ULSTM在在线模式下优于BLSTM；英语到德语任务中，固定大小块交替读写操作的策略表现最佳。
AI.conclusion: ULSTM编码结合固定分段策略可有效提升实时语音翻译的效率与质量，为在线系统设计提供新方向。

title: Star DGT: a Robust Gabor Transform for Speech Denoising
title_zh: Star DGT：用于语音去噪的鲁棒 Gabor 变换
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2103.11233
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2103.11233
abstract: In this paper, we address the speech denoising problem, where Gaussian, pink and blue additive noises are to be removed from a given speech signal. Our approach is based on a redundant, analysis-sparse representation of the original speech signal. We pick an eigenvector of the Zauner unitary matrix and -- under certain assumptions on the ambient dimension -- we use it as window vector to generate a spark deficient Gabor frame. The analysis operator associated with such a frame, is a (highly) redundant Gabor transform, which we use as a sparsifying transform in denoising procedure. We conduct computational experiments on real-world speech data, using as baseline three Gabor transforms generated by state-of-the-art window vectors in time-frequency analysis and compare their performance to the proposed Gabor transform. The results show that our proposed redundant Gabor transform outperforms all others, consistently for all signals.
summary: In this paper, we address the speech denoising problem, where Gaussian, pink and blue additive noises are to be removed from a given speech signal. Our approach is based on a redundant, analysis-sparse representation of the original speech signal. We pick an eigenvector of the Zauner unitary matrix and -- under certain assumptions on the ambient dimension -- we use it as window vector to generate a spark deficient Gabor frame. The analysis operator associated with such a frame, is a (highly) redundant Gabor transform, which we use as a sparsifying transform in denoising procedure. We conduct computational experiments on real-world speech data, using as baseline three Gabor transforms generated by state-of-the-art window vectors in time-frequency analysis and compare their performance to the proposed Gabor transform. The results show that our proposed redundant Gabor transform outperforms all others, consistently for all signals.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们解决了语音去噪问题，即从给定的语音信号中去除高斯、粉红和蓝色加性噪声。我们的方法基于原始语音信号的冗余、分析稀疏表示。我们选择 Zauner 酉矩阵的特征向量，并在环境维度的某些假设下，将其用作窗口向量来生成 Spark 缺陷 Gabor 框架。与此类框架相关的分析算子是（高度）冗余的 Gabor 变换，我们将其用作去噪过程中的稀疏变换。我们对真实世界的语音数据进行计算实验，使用时频分析中最先进的窗口向量生成的三个 Gabor 变换作为基线，并将其性能与所提出的 Gabor 变换进行比较。结果表明，我们提出的冗余 Gabor 变换优于所有其他变换，对于所有信号而言一致。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种稳健的冗余Gabor变换方法，用于去除语音信号中的高斯、粉红和蓝噪声。
AI.motivation: 现有Gabor变换在语音降噪中对不同噪声类型适应性不足，需设计更鲁棒的稀疏表示框架以提升降噪效果。
AI.method: 基于Zauner酉矩阵特征向量构造spark缺陷Gabor框架，利用其冗余分析算子作为稀疏化工具进行降噪处理。
AI.result: 在真实语音数据实验中，所提方法在多种噪声环境下均优于三种先进Gabor变换基的降噪性能。
AI.conclusion: 新方法通过特殊设计的Gabor框架实现了更优的语音降噪效果，验证了冗余稀疏表示在噪声抑制中的有效性。
